title: Longest railroad straight | Guinness World Records
content:
| Category | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Record Holder** | Australian National Railways |
| **Length (km)** | 478 km |
| **Length (miles)** | 297 miles |
| **Location** | Nullarbor Plain, Australia |
| **Start Point** | Mile 496 between Nurina and Loongana, Western Australia |
| **End Point** | Mile 793 between Ooldea and Watson, South Australia |
| **Rail Line Name** | Trans-Australian line |
| **Railway Gauge** | Standard gauge |
| **Completion Date** | 1917 |
| **Dates Surveyed** | Not explicitly stated |
| **Measurement Method** | Not explicitly stated |

**Additional Technical and Contextual Details**
* **Terrain Characteristics:** 
    * The stretch is described as "dead straight, although not level."
    * Crosses a "virtually uninhabited limestone plateau."
    * The word "nullarbor" literally means "no trees."
    * Described as "some of the most forbidding terrain in Australia."
* **Construction Records:**
    * In one year, 712 km (442 miles) of track were laid on the line.
    * A peak of 4 km (2.5 miles) was laid in one day, which is an Australian record.
* **Comparative Data (Secondary Record Mentioned):**
    * The Buenos Aires & Pacific Railway in Argentina is dead straight and almost level for 330 km (205 miles).
    * Located between Junin and MacKenna on the line from Buenos Aires to Mendoza.
    * Gauge for the Argentinian line: 1676 mm (5.49 in).
